question: Please give a 1-minute self-introduction.
Introduce your most representative project and elaborate on your specific contributions.
Why are you applying for this role?
Why are you interested in our company?
Which part of your previous experience best demonstrates your fit for this role?
What is your career plan for the next 3 to 5 years?
What is the most challenging project you have worked on recently?
What do you consider to be your greatest strength?
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate. How did you resolve it?
If a PM or manager proposes a requirement that you think is unreasonable, what would you do?
How do you explain a complex technical concept to a non-technical audience?
Tell me about a time you failed or made a severe mistake. What did you learn and how did you remedy it?
How do you balance 'speed' and 'quality' when working under pressure?
Which programming languages are you most familiar with, and which one do you use most often?
What is the difference between a list and a tuple in Python?
What is time complexity? What are some common time complexities?
What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING in SQL?
Can you explain the difference between INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, and RIGHT JOIN?
What is overfitting, and how do you prevent or solve it?
What is the purpose of train, validation, and test splits?
Explain precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC, and discuss when each is appropriate to use.
What is data leakage in machine learning?
Explain the bias-variance tradeoff.
Write a SQL query to find the first order date for each user.
How would you write a SQL query to calculate DAU (Daily Active Users) over the past 7 days?
Write a SQL query to calculate user next-day retention.
Which SQL window functions have you used? Examples: ROW_NUMBER(), RANK(), LAG()?
How do you define a North Star metric for an e-commerce platform?
If the PM says 'Conversion rate has dropped recently,' how would you approach the analysis?
What is cohort analysis and when should it be used?
What is the general workflow of an A/B test?
What are Type I and Type II errors?
What are the core differences between Random Forest and XGBoost?
Explain the difference between bagging and boosting.
What is the core idea behind SVM, and what is the kernel trick?
What is the principle behind PCA? Why can it be used for dimensionality reduction?
What is the Central Limit Theorem?
How do you deal with class imbalance in a dataset?
If a model performs well on the training set but poorly on the test set, how would you troubleshoot?
How would you design a machine learning model to predict user churn?
What is the difference between feature drift and concept drift?
How do you prevent training-serving skew?
What is the difference between batch inference and real-time inference?
What is shadow deployment and canary deployment?
In deep learning, what occurs when the batch size is too large vs too small?
What is LoRA? Why is it considered resource-efficient?
Explain the general layout and flow of a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system.
What is the difference between an Array and a Linked List?
What are the common differences between Depth-First Search (DFS) and Breadth-First Search (BFS)?
Explain the concept of Dynamic Programming. How does it contrast with a Greedy algorithm?
How would you detect if a linked list has a cycle?
What are the three main pillars of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)?
What is the difference between a GET and a POST HTTP request?
How would you handle high concurrent requests hitting your application layer?
Design a highly scalable rate limiter.
In your most recent project, what was the primary business problem you aimed to solve?
Why did you choose this specific framework or method instead of alternatives in your project?
What was the single most difficult technical challenge you encountered, and how did you overcome it?
If you could do that project all over again, what architectural or modeling step would you change?
How did you guarantee that your project conclusion/metrics were correct and not just anecdotal inferences?
What is the difference between a stack and a queue? Give a real-world use case for each.
Explain hashing and how hash collisions are handled.
What are the SOLID principles? Briefly explain each one.
What is the difference between a process and a thread?
What is a deadlock? How can it be detected and prevented?
What is REST? What are its key constraints and how does it differ from GraphQL?
What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases? When would you choose one over the other?
Explain database indexing. When can an index hurt performance instead of helping?
What is a design pattern? Name two you have used and explain when you applied them.
What is the difference between monolithic and microservices architecture? What are the trade-offs?
How does a load balancer work? What strategies exist for distributing traffic?
What is caching? Compare LRU, write-through, and write-back caching strategies.
Explain the CAP theorem. Can a distributed system satisfy all three properties simultaneously?
What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous programming? When would you use each?
What is Docker? How does containerization differ from virtual machines?
What is CI/CD? Describe the stages of a typical pipeline you have worked with.
What is a binary search tree? What are the time complexities for search, insert, and delete?
Explain the difference between TCP and UDP. When would you choose UDP over TCP?
What is a mutex? How does it differ from a semaphore?
Design a URL shortening service like bit.ly. Walk through your system design decisions.
How would you design a notification system that supports push, email, and SMS at scale?
What is garbage collection? How does Python manage memory?
What is the difference between optimistic locking and pessimistic locking in a database?
Walk me through how you would implement a distributed message queue.
What is eventual consistency, and how does it differ from strong consistency?
What is a feature store, and what problem does it solve in an ML system?
How do you monitor a machine learning model in production? What signals would trigger a retrain?
What is model versioning and why is it critical for reproducibility?
How would you design a data pipeline for a real-time recommendation system?
What is transfer learning and when would you apply it instead of training from scratch?
What is the attention mechanism? Why was it transformative for NLP?
What is the vanishing gradient problem and what techniques address it?
Compare L1 and L2 regularization. How does each affect the model weights?
How do you handle missing data in a machine learning pipeline?
What is feature importance? How do you compute it for tree-based models?
What is an embedding? Give two concrete examples of where embeddings are used in production.
How do you evaluate a recommendation system? What offline and online metrics would you use?
What is quantization in the context of deep learning models? What are its trade-offs?
Explain the difference between model parallelism and data parallelism for distributed training.
What is RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback) and how is it used to align LLMs?
How would you optimize a slow model inference endpoint to meet a 100ms SLA?
How do you prevent data leakage in a time-series forecasting task?
What is a confusion matrix? Walk through precision, recall, and F1 derived from it.
What is online learning? Give an example of a scenario where it is preferred over batch learning.
How would you build a machine learning platform from scratch for a mid-sized company?
What is the difference between a generative model and a discriminative model?
How do you perform A/B testing when deploying a new model, and what metrics do you track?
What is the difference between supervised, unsupervised, and semi-supervised learning?
Explain k-means clustering. What are its assumptions and limitations?
What is hypothesis testing? Explain p-value and statistical significance in plain terms.
What is multicollinearity and how does it affect linear regression?
Compare Lasso and Ridge regression. When would you use each?
How would you perform feature selection for a high-dimensional dataset?
What is cross-validation? Why is k-fold preferred over a single train/test split?
Explain gradient descent and its main variants: batch, stochastic, and mini-batch.
What is a ROC curve? How do you interpret the AUC score?
How would you design an experiment to test whether a new product feature increases user retention?
What is SMOTE? When and why would you use it?
What is time-series decomposition? What are its components?
What is the difference between correlation and causation? How can you establish causality from observational data?
How do you choose between a simpler model and a more complex one for a given problem?
Describe a full end-to-end machine learning project you have worked on, from problem framing to deployment.
What is ensemble learning? Compare voting, bagging, and stacking.
Explain the difference between parametric and non-parametric statistical tests. Give an example of each.
How would you approach building a fraud detection system? What challenges would you anticipate?
What is the curse of dimensionality and why does it matter in machine learning?
What is the difference between a view and a materialized view in SQL?
Explain the difference between GROUP BY and PARTITION BY in SQL.
How would you detect and handle duplicate records in a large dataset?
How would you approach building a KPI dashboard for an executive audience?
What is the difference between mean, median, and mode? When is each the most appropriate measure of center?
How would you calculate customer lifetime value (LTV)? What data would you need?
What is funnel analysis? Walk me through how you would analyze a checkout funnel drop-off.
How would you identify the root cause of a sudden 20% drop in a key metric like daily revenue?
What is a CTE (Common Table Expression) in SQL? When is it preferable to a subquery?
How do you ensure data quality before delivering an analysis? What checks do you perform?
What is the difference between OLTP and OLAP systems? How does this affect your SQL queries?
How would you calculate month-over-month growth rate using only SQL?
What is the difference between a histogram and a bar chart? When do you use each?
How would you measure the impact of a pricing change on overall revenue? Walk me through your analysis plan.
What data visualization tools have you used and what are their respective strengths?
How do you handle a situation where two data sources give conflicting numbers for the same metric?
Explain the concept of seasonality in data. How would you adjust for it in your analysis?
Write a SQL query to find the top 3 products by revenue for each region using window functions.
What is session analysis in web analytics? How would you define a session boundary in SQL?
